    Tsung-Han Yang

    Postdoctoral Researcher @ ORNL
    PhD in Physics @ Brown University

    • Knoxville, TN
    • Email

    • LinkedIn

    Projects

    Project A: Phonon Dynamics in Disordered Systems via RMC

    Methodology: Developed a high-performance numerical engine to calculate vibrational properties from Reverse Monte Carlo (RMC) structural models.

    • Algorithmic Approach: Implemented a Force Constant Matrix (Hessian) construction for large-scale disordered supercells, utilizing Finite Displacement Methods to derive the dynamical matrix.
    • Technical Challenge: Optimized the computation of the Dynamic Structure Factor $S(Q, \omega)$ by parallelizing the Fourier transform of atomic trajectories across $10^5+$ coordinates.
    • Impact: Enables the direct comparison between theoretical atomic models and experimental Inelastic Neutron Scattering (INS) data for non-crystalline materials.
    • Stack: Python, NumPy (Vectorization), SciPy (Sparse Matrices), C++ (Backend kernels).

    Project B
